column2: I decided I would rather risk getting little money if there was a chance I could get a lot of money but also didn't want to risk getting nothing so I didn't go for the highest number. Looking back I should have gone for the highest number to guarantee being the highest figure, but I was too focused on reducing risk as a lot of it was down to luck when it came to getting the random number
column3: Considering the significant difference between £13.62 and £5.73, logical, I think everyone will want to be the higher one. Because if someone lost, he will receive no more than 5.73.
column4: 13.62-6.40=7.22 And that's high enough for me. So I pick the number 120.
column5: I wanted to pick a number above 60 so that even if I received -60 as my random number I would still have a positive result. I landed on 66 because I was okay with losing that amount of money off of 5.73 if I had one of the two lowest scores, and thus I would be happy with both of the outcomes.
